title: Foundations
subtitle: The design tokens, primitives, and rules that everything else in the system is built on. Browse colors, typography, spacing, motion, and the principles behind them.

philosophy.title: Design philosophy
philosophy.subtitle: Six principles inherited from the Mercury system that shape every component decision.

sharp_cards.title: Cards are sharp
sharp_cards.description: Cards use --radius-lg: 0px. The sharpness is the most distinctive signature of the system, contrasting with pill-shaped controls.

pill_controls.title: Controls are pills
pill_controls.description: Buttons and inputs use --radius-pill: 32px. The full-pill geometry makes interactive elements feel approachable against the sharp container chrome.

positive_tracking.title: Positive tracking
positive_tracking.description: Letter-spacing is slightly positive (0.005em). Most dashboards use negative tracking to look modern; we go the opposite way for a calmer, more legible read.

weight_ceiling.title: Weight ceiling at 530
weight_ceiling.description: No font weight ever exceeds 530. Hierarchy is built through size, color, and tracking — never through bold black-on-white shouting.

layering_over_shadow.title: Layering over shadow
layering_over_shadow.description: Elevation is communicated by stacking surface tones (background → surface → surface-raised), not by drop shadows. Shadows exist only for floating overlays.

monochrome_accent.title: Monochrome accent
monochrome_accent.description: The interface is overwhelmingly neutral. Accent color is reserved for primary actions, focus rings, and active states — never for decoration.

colors.title: Colors
colors.subtitle: Surface, text, border, and semantic tokens. Every value adapts to dark/light themes through CSS custom properties.

surfaces.title: Surfaces
surfaces.description: Four surface levels create vertical depth. Stack them — never reach for a shadow first.

text.title: Text
text.description: Four-tier text hierarchy. Pair primary with body copy and headings; reserve subtle for placeholders and disabled state.

borders.title: Borders
borders.description: Lead-based hairlines that read as borders without competing with content. Focus inherits the active accent.

semantic.title: Semantic
semantic.description: Four semantic intents, each with a solid and a muted background companion. Desaturated to feel native to the system.

accent.title: Accent
accent.description: Six accent presets are swappable at runtime via the [data-accent] attribute. Try one — it only re-themes this section, not the rest of the app.

typography.title: Typography
typography.subtitle: Inter for prose, JetBrains Mono for code. Font weights are capped at 530 and tracking runs slightly positive.

families.title: Families
families.description: Inter substitutes Mercury's proprietary arcadia. JetBrains Mono is reserved for monospace contexts: code, token names, tabular numbers.

scale.title: Scale
scale.description: Seven sizes from --fs-xs (11px) to --fs-3xl (32px), each paired with a matching --lh-* line-height token.

weights.title: Weights
weights.description: Four weight tokens, all clamped at 530 or below. The system never goes heavier — the boldest text is still light by conventional standards.

tracking.title: Tracking
tracking.description: Mercury uses subtly POSITIVE tracking (+0.005em on body) instead of the negative tracking common in modern UI. This is one of the most defining personality shifts.
tracking.note: Mercury uses positive tracking. Negative tracking is never used in this system.

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.subtitle: A 4px-based scale from 0 to 64px covering all gaps, padding and margins in the system.
spacing.description: Twelve tokens with predictable doubling (--space-1 through --space-16). Use these tokens directly; avoid arbitrary pixel values.

radii.title: Radii
radii.subtitle: Seven tokens that encode two of Mercury's most distinctive signatures: sharp cards and pill controls.
radii.description: Containers and small elements use small radii (0–4px). Buttons and inputs use --radius-pill (32px) and --radius-pill-lg (40px). Badges and dots use --radius-full.

elevation.title: Elevation
elevation.subtitle: Mercury layers depth through surface tone, not box-shadow. Shadows exist for floating elements only.

layering.title: Surface layering (primary)
layering.description: Four surface tokens stack to create depth: --surface-sunk below, --background as canvas, --surface for cards, --surface-raised for elements that pop forward.

shadows.title: Box shadows (secondary)
shadows.description: --shadow-card is reserved for light theme only — a 1px hairline of contrast, no blur drama. --shadow-pop is for popovers, dropdowns and tooltips in both themes.
shadows.dark_note: No shadow in dark theme — by design.

motion.title: Motion
motion.subtitle: Three durations, two easings. Most UI motion uses --ease-out at --dur-fast or --dur-base.

durations.title: Durations
durations.description: --dur-fast for hover and focus reactions. --dur-base for most state transitions. --dur-slow for overlays entering and leaving.

easings.title: Easings
easings.description: --ease-out for outgoing motion (most UI). --ease-in-out for symmetric transitions like overlays opening and closing.

iconography.title: Iconography
iconography.subtitle: Lucide icons with consistent sizing and stroke-width across the system.

library.title: Library
library.description: Lucide React is the only icon library. Always import from lucide-react and never inline SVGs.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Four canonical sizes: 14px in navigation, 16px inline with text, 20px in buttons and form controls, 24px for display contexts. Stroke-width stays at the Lucide default (2).

localization.title: Localization
localization.description: Token names, hex values, and unit suffixes are intentionally left untranslated — they are part of the design system's vocabulary. Only descriptive prose is localized.
